origin.question:
In a stationary spacetime with a timelike Killing vector field $\xi^\alpha$, previous analysis has established that the integral $Q = \int_\Sigma T^\alpha_ eta \xi^ eta d^3\Sigma_\alpha$ over any spacelike hypersurface $\Sigma$ is independent of the choice of $\Sigma$, thus defining a globally conserved charge (the total internal energy of the source).The gravitational mass of the spacetime can be characterized by the Komar mass, defined via a 2-surface integral at spatial infinity:$$ M_K = - rac{1}{8\pi} \oint_S 
abla^\alpha \xi^ eta dS_{\alpha eta} $$Using the generalized Stokes theorem for antisymmetric tensors in 4-dimensions, $\oint_S F^{\alpha eta} dS_{\alpha eta} = \int_\Sigma 
abla_ eta F^{\alpha eta} d\Sigma_\alpha$, along with the Killing vector identity $
abla_ eta 
abla^\alpha \xi^ eta = R^\alpha_ eta \xi^ eta$ and the Einstein field equations $R_{\alpha eta} -  rac{1}{2}R g_{\alpha eta} = 8\pi T_{\alpha eta}$ (with $(-,+,+,+)$ signature), derive the explicit relationship between the Komar mass $M_K$, the conserved charge $Q$, and the trace of the energy-momentum tensor $T = T^\alpha_\alpha$.
origin.solution:
1. Apply the generalized Stokes theorem to the Komar mass boundary integral, identifying $F^{\alpha eta} = 
abla^\alpha \xi^ eta$:$$ M_K = - rac{1}{8\pi} \oint_S 
abla^\alpha \xi^ eta dS_{\alpha eta} = - rac{1}{8\pi} \int_\Sigma 
abla_ eta (
abla^\alpha \xi^ eta) d^3\Sigma_\alpha $$2. Substitute the Killing vector identity $
abla_ eta 
abla^\alpha \xi^ eta = R^\alpha_ eta \xi^ eta$ into the volume integral:$$ M_K = - rac{1}{8\pi} \int_\Sigma R^\alpha_ eta \xi^ eta d^3\Sigma_\alpha $$3. Use the Einstein field equations $R_{\alpha eta} -  rac{1}{2}R g_{\alpha eta} = 8\pi T_{\alpha eta}$ to express the Ricci tensor in terms of the energy-momentum tensor:$$ R^\alpha_ eta = 8\pi T^\alpha_ eta +  rac{1}{2}R g^\alpha_ eta $$Multiply by $\xi^ eta$:$$ R^\alpha_ eta \xi^ eta = 8\pi T^\alpha_ eta \xi^ eta +  rac{1}{2}R \xi^\alpha $$4. Substitute this back into the expression for $M_K$:$$ M_K = - rac{1}{8\pi} \int_\Sigma \left( 8\pi T^\alpha_ eta \xi^ eta +  rac{1}{2}R \xi^\alphaight) d^3\Sigma_\alpha = - \int_\Sigma T^\alpha_ eta \xi^ eta d^3\Sigma_\alpha -  rac{1}{16\pi} \int_\Sigma R \xi^\alpha d^3\Sigma_\alpha $$5. Express the Ricci scalar $R$ in terms of the trace $T$. Contracting the Einstein equations with $g^{\alpha eta}$ in the $(-,+,+,+)$ convention yields:$$ R - 2R = 8\pi T \implies -R = 8\pi T \implies R = -8\pi T $$6. Substitute $R = -8\pi T$ into the integral:$$ M_K = - \int_\Sigma T^\alpha_ eta \xi^ eta d^3\Sigma_\alpha -  rac{1}{16\pi} \int_\Sigma (-8\pi T) \xi^\alpha d^3\Sigma_\alpha $$$$ M_K = - \int_\Sigma T^\alpha_ eta \xi^ eta d^3\Sigma_\alpha +  rac{1}{2} \int_\Sigma T \xi^\alpha d^3\Sigma_\alpha $$7. Recognizing the first term as the conserved charge $Q$ defined in the premise, we arrive at the final relation:$$ M_K = -Q +  rac{1}{2} \int_\Sigma T \xi^\alpha d^3\Sigma_\alpha $$
